old_claim.note: Early Copyright & Printing Privileges is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim_absent: No copyright-privilege dependency remains because the scoped artifact is the printed quarto itself, whose existence requires theater content and printing but not that optional legal context.

source_shape.source_locator: The object record gives the 1594 imprint, sellers, title-page text, and performed companies without identifying a privilege as constitutive of the edition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The surviving quarto is documented through its physical imprint and theatrical title-page evidence.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later Hamlet register entry cannot establish copyright privilege as necessary to the earlier Titus printed artifact.

ontology_before: Printed play quartos carried a direct legal-context edge based on a different play's later registration.
ontology_after: The artifact depends on theater and printing; Stationers' rights remain optional publication context.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_theater_scripts no longer directly depends on early_copyright_printing_privileges.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to licensed editions and evidence showed a privilege was required for every scoped quarto.
short_reason: A printing privilege was not constitutive of the 1594 artifact.
